message:
chore(lints): silence three pre-existing clippy errors + apply cargo fmt

`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` was flagging three
pre-existing issues that blocked any CI configured with `-D warnings`:

1. **`unnecessary_sort_by` (src/main.rs:202)** — backup-list sort was
   `sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(&a.1))` (manual reverse). Rewrote as
   `sort_by_key(|b| std::cmp::Reverse(b.1))` per the lint's auto-fix
   suggestion. Identical semantics, fewer cycles, no closure capture.

2. **`collapsible_match` (src/main.rs:1704, 1714)** — two adjacent match
   arms inside the hidapi device-walk loop were
   `PID => { if !found_*_usb && !found_*_hid { … } }`. Both collapse
   cleanly to `PID if !found_*_usb && !found_*_hid => { … }` match
   guards, dropping one indentation level.

Also applied `cargo fmt` to the workspace, which cleaned up
pre-existing rustfmt drift in `src/main.rs` (the `fs::read_dir(...)`
chain in `list_backups` had an over-long binding line) and
`tests/cli_validation.rs` (a handful of `assert!` and
`predicate::str::contains(...)` calls were single-line over the line
limit).

`cargo test`: 47 lib + 101 integration pass.
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean.
`cargo fmt --check`: clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
